display.purpose: Page titles — one per page. Tight tracking, commanding weight. H1 on home, blog posts, case studies.

headline.purpose: Section headings on index pages (Blog, Portfolio listing).

title.purpose: Card titles, project names, subsection headings within prose.

body.purpose: All prose content. Max 70ch line length. Comfortable for extended reading.

label.purpose: Badge text, metadata labels, date stamps. All-caps, tight, discrete.

mono.purpose: Code blocks, technical snippets, file paths. Only in prose areas.

shadows.name: hover-lift
shadows.purpose: Subtle hover lift for interactive cards. Controlled rise, not a float.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Default duration and easing for all state transitions (hover, focus, color change).

motion.name: pulse-slow
motion.purpose: Slow pulse for the availability status dot only.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary call-to-action button. High contrast, unmistakable. Used for 'View work', 'Resume', and primary navigation CTAs.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: oklch(0.82 0.008 260); color: oklch(0.05 0.008 260); padding: 8px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 2px; font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: opacity 150ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { opacity: 0.9; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px oklch(0.62 0.09 220); }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary action. Subtle border treatment. Used alongside primary buttons for less prominent CTAs.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { background: transparent; color: oklch(0.82 0.008 260); padding: 8px 16px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.20 0.015 260); border-radius: 2px; font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: oklch(0.20 0.015 260 / 0.3); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px oklch(0.62 0.09 220); }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Tertiary action. No border, minimal footprint. Used for 'View all' links styled as buttons.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: oklch(0.82 0.008 260); padding: 8px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 2px; font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: oklch(0.20 0.015 260 / 0.3); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px oklch(0.62 0.09 220); }

components.name: Project Card
components.description: Project showcase card. Used in the portfolio grid and featured section on the home page.
components.html:
Echo
Founder & Lead Developer

AI journaling app using local LLM inference and RAG. Reached 100+ users.

ReactLangChainPostgreSQL
View case study →
components.css: .ds-card { background: oklch(0.09 0.01 260); border: 1px solid oklch(0.20 0.015 260); border-radius: 2px; padding: 24px; transition: background 150ms ease-out, box-shadow 150ms ease-out; display: flex; flex-direction: column; } .ds-card:hover { background: oklch(0.13 0.012 260); box-shadow: 0 2px 8px oklch(0 0 0 / 0.25); } .ds-card-header { margin-bottom: 16px; } .ds-card-title { font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: clamp(1.125rem, 2vw, 1.5rem); font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.3; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: oklch(0.82 0.008 260); } .ds-card-role { font-size: 0.875rem; color: oklch(0.55 0.02 250); margin-top: 4px; } .ds-card-content { flex: 1; } .ds-card-desc { font-size: 0.875rem; color: oklch(0.55 0.02 250); line-height: 1.6; margin-bottom: 12px; } .ds-card-tags { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px; } .ds-badge { font-size: 0.625rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: oklch(0.82 0.008 260); background: oklch(0.20 0.015 260 / 0.3); padding: 2px 6px; border-radius: 2px; } .ds-card-footer { margin-top: 16px; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.82 0.008 260); } .ds-card-footer:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

components.name: Desktop Navigation
components.description: Top navigation bar. Horizontal row of page links with active state indicator.
components.html:
JK
HomeProjectsWriting
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; width: 100%; } .ds-nav-logo { font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: clamp(1.125rem, 2vw, 1.5rem); font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: oklch(0.82 0.008 260); text-decoration: none; } .ds-nav-links { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 24px; } .ds-nav-link { font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.55 0.02 250); text-decoration: none; transition: color 150ms ease-out; } .ds-nav-link:hover, .ds-nav-link-active { color: oklch(0.82 0.008 260); } .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px oklch(0.62 0.09 220); border-radius: 2px; }

components.name: Reading Progress
components.description: Fixed reading progress bar at top of case study and blog post pages. Uses the Docklight accent.
components.css: .ds-progress-track { position: fixed; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; height: 4px; z-index: 50; } .ds-progress-fill { height: 100%; background: oklch(0.62 0.09 220); transition: width 150ms ease-out; }

components.name: Blog Post Link
components.description: A blog post entry in the index list. Title with date on the same line.
components.html: Why AI needs better interfacesMay 2026
components.css: .ds-blog-entry { padding: 4px 0; } .ds-blog-link { text-decoration: none; display: block; } .ds-blog-row { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: baseline; gap: 16px; } .ds-blog-title { font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.82 0.008 260); transition: text-decoration 150ms ease-out; } .ds-blog-link:hover .ds-blog-title { text-decoration: underline; } .ds-blog-date { font-size: 0.875rem; color: oklch(0.55 0.02 250); font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; white-space: nowrap; }

narrative.overview:
A dark, focused workspace where craftsmanship takes center stage. This is the portfolio equivalent of a developer's terminal at midnight — deep background, soft luminous text, and color used with surgical precision. The design recedes so the work occupies the foreground.

The system borrows its ethos from dark code editor themes (midnight.nvim specifically): healthy contrast without eye strain, semantic clarity without visual noise, and a cooler neutral palette punctuated by intentional accent warmth. It is emphatically not a generic dark-mode portfolio — the background is a deep cool slate, not pure black. The text is a soft blue-gray white, not harsh pure white. Every value is tuned for longevity: the site should be comfortable to read for extended sessions, not just glance at.

What this system explicitly rejects: generic dev-portfolio templates (avatar heroes, skill bars, gradient sections, typing animations), SaaS dashboard aesthetics, glassmorphism, gradient text, side-stripe borders, and any element that screams 'framework template.' If someone could identify the boilerplate at a glance, it's failed.
keyCharacteristics: Dark-focused: cool slate background, soft luminous foreground
Restrained palette: tinted neutrals with a single muted teal accent (≤10% surface)
Flat by default: tonal layering for depth, near-zero borders at rest
Terminal-adjacent: high legibility, low saturation, zero decoration
Content-forward: the work speaks; the UI is transparent

rules.name: The Docklight Rule
rules.body: The accent appears on ≤10% of any screen. Its rarity is the point — when it appears, the user knows something is actionable. If the accent feels like a UI chrome color, it's being overused.

rules.name: The No-Black, No-White Rule
rules.body: Pure #000 and #fff are forbidden. Every 'black' is a deep cool slate; every 'white' is a soft blue-gray. This prevents visual crush and eye strain during extended reading.

rules.name: The Single-Face Rule
rules.body: No serif, no display font, no contrast pairing. Hierarchy is achieved entirely through weight, size, and spacing — not by switching typefaces. The discipline of a single variable font is the point.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Shadows appear only as a response to state (hover, interaction). A resting surface with a shadow is a bug.

dos: Do use the Docklight accent on interactive elements only — links, focus rings, progress bar.
Do use tonal layering for surface hierarchy: background → surface → elevated.
Do use 2px radius for all corners. The sharpness is intentional.
Do let the content breathe. Generous spacing between sections (48px+), tight spacing within components.
Do use weight and size for typographic hierarchy. Never use color to differentiate heading levels.
Do keep body text between 65–75 characters per line.
Do test every screen in both light and dark mode if light mode is maintained.
donts: Don't use pure #000 or #fff. Every surface has a subtle chromatic lean.
Don't use side-stripe borders (thick border-left as accent). Full borders or nothing.
Don't use gradient text, glassmorphism, or the hero-metric template.
Don't use identical card grids with icon + heading + text, repeated endlessly.
Don't use skill bars, avatar heroes, typing animations, or skill-tag grids.
Don't add decorative borders, ornamental flourishes, or stock imagery.
Don't animate CSS layout properties. Animate opacity and transforms only.
Don't use bounce or elastic easing. Use ease-out exponential curves.
Don't open modals as a first interaction pattern.
